summary: On Day 3 post-extraction for a painful but non-swollen/feverish socket, treat by irrigating it with sterile fluids like saline/chlorhexidine followed by placing an analgesic/zinc oxide eugelone dressing. This addresses potential dry socket while minimizing infection risk given current stable condition. Agent1 reasoned that based on typical presentations of alveolar osteitis ("dry socket"), standard treatments involving cleaning/saline rinse plus protective dressings are indicated immediately despite lack of systemic symptoms. Key steps include gentle debridement if needed then applying appropriate local care measures aimed at reducing discomfort & promoting healing until natural resolution occurs over days-weeks depending upon individual response/recovery timeline.
